terms.etiology_clue: Sporadic GNAS somatic mutation causing replacement of normal bone with fibrous connective tissue and woven bone; ground glass appearance
terms.management_clue: Conservative monitoring when stable; surgical recontouring for functional or aesthetic impairment; bisphosphonates for pain control

terms.etiology_clue: Accumulation of fluid between the reduced enamel epithelium and crown of an unerupted tooth; most common developmental odontogenic cyst
terms.management_clue: Enucleation with extraction of associated impacted tooth; marsupialisation followed by enucleation for large cysts in growing patients

terms.etiology_clue: Proliferation of epithelial rests of Malassez stimulated by periapical inflammatory mediators secondary to pulp necrosis; most common odontogenic cyst
terms.management_clue: Root canal treatment for small lesions; surgical enucleation with apicoectomy or extraction for larger cysts; histopathologic confirmation required

terms.etiology_clue: Associated with PTCH1 tumour suppressor gene mutation; parakeratinised stratified squamous epithelium lining with high recurrence potential due to satellite cysts
terms.management_clue: Enucleation with Carnoy's solution or peripheral ostectomy; decompression for large lesions; long-term annual radiographic follow-up for recurrence

terms.etiology_clue: Hamartomatous odontogenic malformation; compound type resembles rudimentary teeth, complex type forms disorganised calcified mass; most common odontogenic tumour
terms.management_clue: Conservative surgical excision; extraction of associated impacted tooth if present; excellent prognosis with negligible recurrence risk

terms.etiology_clue: Arises from remnants of odontogenic epithelium; locally aggressive benign tumour with multilocular soap-bubble pattern and root resorption; BRAF V600E mutation common
terms.management_clue: Wide surgical resection with adequate bony margins; reconstruction with bone graft or plate; lifelong radiographic surveillance due to high recurrence rate
